label: Flat Ridge 2 tornado — initial claim submitted (gross, pre-negotiation)
source_denominator_or_scope: 204 GE WTGs, 66,000-acre KS farm (Barber/Harper/Kingman/Sumner Cos.); event 2012-05-19; MW capacity NOT stated in filing
useful_for: single-event tornado gross severity anchor (farm-level, no MW denom)
caveats: MW capacity NOT in the court filing. Public sources associate 'Flat Ridge 2' with a ~419.6 MW BP project but that figure is NOT in the primary document -> $/MW normalization WITHHELD. Coverage-dispute figures reflect negotiated settlement, not a clean loss adjustment.

label: Flat Ridge 2 tornado — adjuster total-loss estimate
source_denominator_or_scope: Same event; adjuster's own estimate 'in excess of $12M'; MW NOT stated
useful_for: upper-bound envelope for a direct tornado strike on a large farm
caveats: Estimate, not paid. Gap between $12M adjuster estimate and $7.25M paid is a coverage-dispute artifact. No MW denominator.

label: Flat Ridge 2 tornado — total paid net of deductible
reported_unit: net USD after $250,000 deductible; two partial payments $3.5M + $3.75M
source_denominator_or_scope: Same event; net-of-deductible settlement; MW NOT stated
useful_for: single-event tornado NET severity anchor (farm-level, no MW denom)
caveats: Net-of-deductible negotiated settlement under litigation. Underwriters separately accepted unallocated proofs of loss ~$7.5M. No MW denominator -> not normalizable.

label: Flat Ridge 2 — annual policy premium (context)
source_denominator_or_scope: Same policy; premium not a loss figure
useful_for: premium-to-loss context only; NOT a severity benchmark
caveats: Premium, not loss. Cited for context on the risk-transfer economics of the same farm; do not treat as a loss datum.

label: Flat Ridge 2 net paid — DERIVED $/MW sensitivity (unconfirmed 419.6 MW)
source_denominator_or_scope: $7.25M net / 419.6 MW (BP-project association, NOT in filing)
useful_for: illustrative fleet-average $/MW ONLY if MW is later confirmed
caveats: DERIVED sensitivity, NOT a benchmark. Denominator (419.6 MW) is a public project association absent from the primary filing. normalized_usd_per_MW deliberately BLANK per golden rule. A farm-average $/MW radically understates the per-struck-turbine severity because a tornado hits a narrow corridor, not the whole farm.

label: Greenfield IA EF4 tornado — whole-event property damage (NOT turbine-specific)
reported_unit: whole-tornado total USD (homes + turbines + all structures)
source_denominator_or_scope: Adair County IA, 2024-05-21; 5-10 turbines collapsed (Orient/Arbor Hill/Prescott); total is residential-dominated
useful_for: event context / turbine-count anchor; NOT a turbine severity number
caveats: $31.8M is the WHOLE-tornado total, dominated by residential Greenfield damage, NOT isolated to turbines. No turbine-specific dollar figure exists. Cannot be normalized to wind $/MW.

label: Greenfield EF4 — official peak wind speed (intensity anchor)
reported_unit: mph, EF4 official rating (DOW radar measured 309-318 mph in-flow)
source_denominator_or_scope: Official NWS EF-rating; forensic (Marshall) est 120-140 mph at turbine sites; FEA ~170 mph
useful_for: intensity ordinate to place tornado turbine failures on the DR curve x-axis (V_3s vs Ve50)
caveats: Wide spread between official EF (185 mph), DOW in-flow (309-318 mph), and site-specific forensic estimates (120-170 mph). Intensity, not loss. Use as a curve x-axis anchor with an explicit uncertainty band, not a point.

label: Benton/Fowler IN EF2 — turbine snapped, blade-throw 600 yd
source_denominator_or_scope: Fowler IN area, 2023-03-31, EF2 (~115 mph); NCEI event 1085924
useful_for: tower-buckling failure-mode confirmation; debris safety-zone metric
caveats: Damage-state + debris distance only; no dollar figure. Blade-throw 600 yd is an exposure/safety metric, not a severity number.

label: Harper County KS EF3 (2012) — blades removed from several turbines
source_denominator_or_scope: Harper Co KS, 2012-05-19 EF3; EPRI-cited with storm-chase video documentation
useful_for: blade-subsystem failure mode at EF3; maps to WT_BLADE_STRUCT
caveats: Blade-subsystem failure (not tower collapse). No dollar figure. Same storm system/day as Flat Ridge 2.

label: 2020 Midwest Derecho — MidAmerican wind fleet NULL damage (measured 126 mph gust)
source_denominator_or_scope: 2020-08-10 derecho across IA; MidAmerican 'did not sustain any significant wind farm damage'; 126 mph gust measured
useful_for: straight-line-wind NULL anchor: extreme SLW event with no fleet damage -> lower bound / D50 baseline confirmation
caveats: A genuine measured near-zero, NOT a missing value. Confirms modern turbines survive an extreme (126 mph) straight-line gust with feathering/shutdown. Later Sept/Oct blade failures near Stuart were attributed to LIGHTNING, not wind (Arbor Hills). Do not code the lightning failures as SLW loss.

label: Arbor Hills blade failure (Sept 2020) — attributed to lightning, NOT wind
source_denominator_or_scope: Stuart IA, Sept 2020 (temporally near the Aug derecho)
useful_for: attribution discipline example: do NOT fold this into SLW loss
caveats: CONTEXT_NOT_BENCHMARK: forensic cause is lightning, not straight-line wind, despite temporal proximity to the derecho. Included to prevent double-counting/mis-attribution.

label: Bouchard & Romanic 2023 — tornado fragility logistic (German turbines)
source_denominator_or_scope: Peer-reviewed; German turbine stock; collapse threshold ~58 m/s
useful_for: curve SHAPE/method analog for the canonical logistic DR form; NOT a US plug-in
caveats: Shape/method analog only. German turbine class + European wind climate; do NOT plug parameters directly into the US IEC-II-anchored curve. Provides independent evidence the logistic form is appropriate.

label: Bouchard & Romanic 2023 — replacement value basis (1,250 EUR/kW)
source_denominator_or_scope: German cost basis; full-loss threshold D>=0.7; tower share ~30% (26.3% in 16-component table)
useful_for: independent (non-US) cross-check of the subsystem cost-share ladder
caveats: European (EUR) cost basis; component granularity differs from NREL CWER. Cross-check only, not a substitute for the canonical $1,968,000/MW ladder. Tower 26.3% / Rotor blades 22.2% component shares broadly consistent with upstream.

label: Bouchard & Romanic 2023 — AGG/OCC ~EUR 25M (modeled portfolio)
source_denominator_or_scope: Modeled German portfolio; not a US industry AAL
useful_for: order-of-magnitude portfolio tornado AAL/OCC shape (non-US)
caveats: Modeled, European, portfolio-specific. Different metric family (annualized/portfolio) from the event anchors above -> keep on a SEPARATE axis. Not a US benchmark.

label: FEMA NRI v1.20 — Atlanta 11-county Tornado EAL (all-asset, not turbine)
reported_unit: annualized expected loss USD, all building stock
source_denominator_or_scope: Atlanta 11-county region; Tornado layer; ALL exposure (buildings/pop/ag), NOT wind turbines
useful_for: demonstrates FEMA keeps Tornado and Strong Wind as SEPARATE peril layers (mirrors our [T]/[W] split)
caveats: All-asset regional EAL, NOT turbine-specific and NOT normalizable to wind $/MW. Cited to justify the sub-peril split, not as a turbine loss.

label: FEMA NRI v1.20 — Atlanta 11-county Strong Wind EAL (all-asset)
reported_unit: annualized expected loss USD, all building stock
source_denominator_or_scope: Atlanta 11-county region; Strong Wind layer; ALL exposure, NOT wind turbines
useful_for: Tornado:Strong-Wind EAL ratio (~27:1 here) as a rough peril-mix signal for this region
caveats: All-asset regional EAL, NOT turbine-specific. The ~27:1 Tornado:Strong-Wind ratio is region-specific (Atlanta) and building-stock-weighted, not transferable to a turbine portfolio.

label: GCube — 2019 weather-related solar loss ~2400% higher than non-weather
source_denominator_or_scope: GCube renewables book; SOLAR-heavy framing; wind mentioned qualitatively (flood>blade)
useful_for: peril-share CONTEXT only; establishes weather as dominant renewables loss driver
caveats: Solar-heavy blended framing; NOT wind-turbine convective-wind severity. No TIV denominators. Downgraded to context.

label: Moody's — US wind PML +58% over hard market (QUARANTINE: hurricane-dominated)
source_denominator_or_scope: Reinsurer-level 'US wind' property-cat PML — dominated by HURRICANE, not SCS wind
useful_for: NOTHING for this pair beyond illustrating hurricane contamination
caveats: QUARANTINE / CONTEXT_NOT_BENCHMARK. 'US wind PML' here is the all-peril property-cat wind PML dominated by tropical cyclone, and is reinsurer-level (not ground-up asset loss). Explicitly fenced OUT of the convective-wind cell.

label: Moody's RMS NA SCS model — hail-dominant AAL framing (blended)
source_denominator_or_scope: NA Severe Convective Storm model; hail+tornado+SLW combined product; hail-dominant
useful_for: confirms SCS AAL is hail-dominated -> tornado/SLW is a minority slice of blended SCS numbers
caveats: Blended SCS product; hail-dominant. Any headline SCS AAL over-states the tornado/SLW share. Magnitude gated. Use only to caution against reading blended SCS numbers as wind.

label: NREL CWER 2024 — land-based wind installed CapEx (value ladder)
source_denominator_or_scope: NREL 2024 Cost of Wind Energy Review reference project; primary-source CapEx table
useful_for: THE installed-TIV denominator; matches upstream Damage_Modeling value ladder line-for-line
caveats: Denominator/value basis, NOT a loss. Component $/kW (Blades 282, Tower 276, Foundation 120...) match the canonical solar_wind_value_breakdown.xlsx rollup. This is the anchor all $/MW normalization uses.

label: Damage_Modeling / NREL — physical replaceable value (value ladder)
source_denominator_or_scope: Upstream canonical value ladder (wind_tornado_wind cell); physical replaceable base
useful_for: THE physical-replaceable denominator for failure-unit damage-ratio crosswalk
caveats: Denominator/value basis, NOT a loss. Physical replaceable value < installed TIV (excludes soft/dev costs). Failure-unit value shares (BLADE 17.3%, TOWER 16.9%, NACELLE 34.5%, FOUNDATION 6.2%, POWER_ELEC 3.7%) are measured against THIS base.

label: LBNL — land-based wind installed cost time-series (denominator cross-check)
source_denominator_or_scope: LBNL Land-Based Wind Market Report; built-project averages (vs NREL modeled reference)
useful_for: denominator volatility / cross-check on the $1,968/kW modeled reference
caveats: Built-project average vs NREL modeled reference -> the $1,370-1,600/kW range vs $1,968/kW gap is a scope difference (per golden rule), not an error. Cross-check only; the crosswalk uses the NREL/upstream $1,968,000/MW anchor.

label: US Wind Turbine Database — onshore fleet exposure (denominator)
source_denominator_or_scope: USWTDB national onshore fleet; illustrative fleet TIV ~$314B at $1,968,000/MW
useful_for: fleet-level exposure denominator for any portfolio-share reasoning
caveats: Exposure count, NOT a loss. The ~$314B fleet TIV is illustrative order-of-magnitude only, not a validated aggregate. No convective-wind loss attached.

label: IEC 61400 Class II — Ve50 extreme 50-yr gust (curve x-axis anchor)
reported_unit: m/s / mph, IEC Class II (default class)
source_denominator_or_scope: IEC 61400-1 design classes: I=70 m/s, II=59.5 m/s (default), III=52.5 m/s
useful_for: the denominator of the canonical hazard axis X_SPEED_RATIO_TO_IEC (r = V_3s_hub / Ve50)
caveats: Design threshold, NOT a loss. Class II (Ve50=59.5 m/s) is the cell's default. The DR curve is expressed in ratio-to-Ve50, so this is the normalization constant, not a benchmark.

label: IEC/operational — turbine cut-out / shutdown threshold (~55 mph)
source_denominator_or_scope: DOE: turbines feather/lock blades and shut down above ~55 mph to shed load
useful_for: explains the MidAmerican null result: feathering at 55 mph precedes the 126 mph gust survival
caveats: Operational threshold, NOT a loss. Below the design/damage regime; explains why sub-design SLW events produce near-zero damage (D50 baseline).

label: NIST/aeroelastic — tower displacement-based fragility method (curve form)
reported_value: bounded logistic on speed ratio (steel vs concrete, 90-122 m hub)
source_denominator_or_scope: FE/aeroelastic displacement-based fragility; hub heights 90-122 m
useful_for: corroborates canonical WT_TOWER_STRUCT curve form (bounded_logistic_on_speed_ratio, D50=1.48)
caveats: Method/form corroboration, NOT a loss or a US-fleet parameter set. Supports the choice of logistic-on-ratio form for the tower failure unit.

label: Greenfield DOW radar — in-flow wind speed (intensity uncertainty)
source_denominator_or_scope: Greenfield IA EF4 2024-05-21; DOW radar; vs 185 mph official EF surface rating
useful_for: upper bound on the intensity-uncertainty band for the Greenfield turbine failures
caveats: Radar in-flow aloft, NOT the 3-s gust at hub height that drives the curve. Large gap vs surface EF rating illustrates why tornado intensity->DR mapping carries a wide band. Intensity, not loss.
